query: Coinbase job posting (artificial intelligence OR machine learning OR generative AI OR LLM OR data scientist)

results.title: Senior Staff Software Engineer, AI Developer Experience at Coinbase | Gravity Engineering
highlights:
Senior Staff Software Engineer, AI Developer Experience at Coinbase | Gravity Engineering

## About the Role

Coinbase is on a mission to increase economic freedom for more than 1 billion people. The company is accelerating its strategic commitment to AI-driven development, believing that the deep integration of artificial intelligence will fundamentally rewrite the laws of software engineering efficiency. The Developer Experience (DevX) organization is tasked with turning this conviction into reality.

Coinbase is looking for a highly visionary, and hands-on, Principal Software Engineer to lead the technical evolution of software development at Coinbase. While DevX leadership focuses on organizational scale, this role will be one of the key architects and technical pioneers driving Coinbase’s advancement in AI-driven engineering.

In this role, you will determine how to leverage this technology to not just enhance workflows, but to reimagine them for the next decade. You will design and build the architecture for a powerful, intelligent ecosystem for thousands of engineers, blending resilient infrastructure fundamentals with cutting-edge AI agents. Your goal is to build the tools and systems that enable our teams to move faster and more reliably than any other engineering organization in the industry, shifting focus from routine implementation to high-value architecture and product creation.

## What you’ll do (job duties)

- Architect an AI-First Ecosystem: Deeply embed AI agents into the development loop - automating test generation, refactoring legacy code, proactively detecting bugs, and optimizing performance before code ever hits production.
- Pioneer LLM Integration: Execute on our strong thesis on how LLMs and agents will rewrite the SDLC. You will prototype, build, and deploy the next generation of autonomous engineering tools.
- Bridge the Gap Between AI and Fundamentals: You understand that advanced AI tools require a stable, rigorously tested underlying architecture. You will partner closely with infrastructure counterparts to ensure AI tools scale securely and performantly.
- Treat Platform as a Product: Guide the technical direction to build and scale a best-in-class Internal Developer Platform that treats internal engineers as customers. The platform must be an enabler of speed, removing friction so engineers can focus on solving crypto’s hardest problems.
- Define the AI-Native Engineering Standard: As AI handles more implementation tasks, you will help define the changing workflows, technical standards, and best practices required for the next generation of engineering at Coinbase.

## What we look for in you (job requirements)

- Deep AI Conviction & Application: You have a strong thesis on how LLMs and agents will rewrite the SDLC. You have experience or a clear plan for practically applying AI to solve engineering bottlenecks.
- Principal-Level Technical Authority: Deep technical judgment in engineering fundamentals - CI/CD, public cloud (AWS/GCP), container orchestration, and modern testing methodologies. You have a proven track record of driving cross-organizational architectural shifts that impact thousands of engineers’ productivity.
- Product Mindset: You possess deep empathy for developers. You treat the platform as a product, consistently gathering feedback and iterating to reduce friction and improve user satisfaction.
- AI Agents: Direct experience deploying internal AI assistants, "Copilot-at-scale" initiatives, or autonomous coding agents.
- Quality at Speed: You know how to leverage automation to drive high velocity without sacrificing quality. You design systems where security and reliability are standard, enabling frequent, safe deployments at massive scale.

## Nice to haves

- Previous experience at a fast-paced, high-growth stage internet/software company.

## View Assessment Process

## Think you'll be a good fit?

# Principal Software Engineer, AI Automation
[...]
At Coinbase, our mission is to increase economic freedom in the world. It’s a massive, ambitious opportunity that demands the best of us, every day, as we build the emerging onchain platform — and with it, the future global financial system.

At Coinbase, our mission is to increase economic freedom in the world. To achieve this, we are becoming an "AI-first" company and fundamentally reimagining how a global financial institution operates. AI is at the center of this strategy, driving our core processes while human experts serve as the "function calls" that the system consults for high-level judgment.The Role
[...]

You will build Agentic AI systems that will power our multiple org-wide initiatives and their primitives, orchestration layers, and the tools that will enable every team at Coinbase to automate their workflows. You will report to the VP, Engineering and will operate with the highest level of autonomy to define what a fully AI-driven company looks like from the ground up.What You’ll Do

results.title: Senior Data Scientist, Platform (Integrity) (Remote) | Wfh
highlights:
Senior Data Scientist, Platform (Integrity) (Remote) | Wfh
[...]
# Senior Data Scientist, Platform (Integrity) (Remote)
[...]
Senior Data Scientist, Platform (Integrity)
[...]
Department: Data Science
[...]
Trust is the foundation of a vibrant Airbnb community. The Content Integrity Data Science team helps build and protect that trust by ensuring that the content on Airbnb, including listings, profiles, messages and other user-generated experiences, is accurate, authentic and aligned with our policies and community standards.
[...]
We partner closely with product, engineering, policy and operations teams to develop advanced AI-driven content understanding systems, scale effective human-in-the-loop workflows and proactively defend against emerging content risks, so guests and hosts can interact with confidence.
[...]
This role is unique in that it directly improves the safety, trust, and quality of real-world user experiences by advancing Airbnb’s ability to understand, interpret and act on content at scale. You will help shape how the platform reasons about listings, profiles, messages and other user-generated content by building the next generation of Trust Content Understanding Models.
[...]
- The ideal candidate is a motivated and talented “full-stack” Data Scientist with strong applied ML intuition and a bias toward impact, who can own and drive forward challenging, high-visibility initiatives such as:
[...]
- Advance Airbnb’s content integrity capabilities by building Natural Language Processing (NLP) and LLM-based models that understand intent, policy compliance, quality and risk across listings, profiles, and user communications
[...]
- Develop high-performing models for detecting problematic or misleading content, including text classification, semantic similarity, information extraction and generative model-based reasoning for policy interpretation and enforcement
[...]
- Design and optimize human-in-the-loop Machine Learning (ML) systems for content review, labeling, escalation and continuous model improvement
[...]
- Build systems to detect emerging content risks and abuse patterns across regions, cohorts and surfaces using statistical, ML and representation-learning approaches
[...]
- Artificial Intelligence / Machine Learning: Build and deploy production AI/ML systems for content integrity and trust content understanding, including feature engineering, model development and evaluation, thresholding, error analysis and end-to-end model lifecycle management. This includes working with NLP and LLM-based models in real production settings.
[...]
- Inference: Partner with inference data scientists to conduct rigorous quantitative analyses, applying working knowledge of causal inference to interpret results, assess impact, and identify gaps and opportunities to improve content quality and trust outcomes.
[...]
- Your Expertise:
[...]
- 5+ years of industry experience in a quantitative analysis role with a Master’s degree in a quantitative field (computer science, statistics etc.), or 2+ years of experience with a Ph.D.
[...]
- State-of-the-art knowledge of AI/ML models
- Hands-on experience building, evaluating, and deploying NLP and LLM-based solutions, including text classification, information extraction, semantic understanding or generative applications.
[...]
- Working knowledge of causal inference
[...]
This position is US - Remote Eligible. The role may include occasional work at an Airbnb office or attendance at offsites, as agreed to with your manager. While the position is Remote Eligible, you must live in a state where Airbnb, Inc. has a registered entity. Click here for the up-to-date list of excluded states. This list is continuously evolving, so please check back with us if the state you live in is on the exclusion list. If your position is employed by another Airbnb entity, your recruiter will inform you what states you are eligible to work from.

# Staff Software Engineer, Backend (Consumer - Risk) - Coinbase
[...]
As a Staff Software Engineer on the Risk Engineering team within the Consumer & Business group, you'll serve as the technical anchor for the team that protects Coinbase and millions of users from fraud, financial crime, and financial risk. You'll define multi-quarter technical strategies, shape the architecture of our most critical risk systems, and lead the engineering team that builds real-time detection, decisioning, and mitigation infrastructure operating at the scale and speed of crypto markets. Your architectural decisions will determine how effectively we protect users and the business as Coinbase scales globally.

highlights:

[...]
# Staff Software Engineer, EAA CX - Coinbase
[...]
## Staff Software Engineer (EAA)
[...]
The Customer Administration Engineering (CAE) team within Coinbase's Enterprise Applications and Architecture org builds and operates Control Center, the secure platform that powers customer data operations for approximately 7,000 internal users across CX, Compliance, Legal, Global Investigations, and Trust and Risk. As a Staff Software Engineer, you'll be a technical anchor for the team, driving the evolution of Control Center from a human-specialist UI into a modular, AI-agent-ready operations platform that exposes capabilities via MCP for both human and autonomous agentic consumers. You'll lead architectural decisions, mentor engineers, and deliver high-impact, cross-cutting platform capabilities at an exciting inflection point for the product.
[...]
- Own the architecture and delivery of foundational platform capabilities including MCP tool registries, AI orchestration layers, risk-based access automation, and the away-team contribution model that enables product groups to build on Control Center safely and at speed.
[...]
- Drive the evolution of Control Center into an AI-agent-ready, headless operations platform by designing and implementing MCP procedure constructs, agentic guardrails, HITL orchestration, and evaluation frameworks for secure autonomous operations.
[...]
- Build highly reliable, secure distributed backend services, owning SLO definitions, observability instrumentation, incident response, and operational readiness for Tier-1 systems serving thousands of internal users and AI agents.
[...]
- 8+ years of software engineering experience with a demonstrated track record architecting and delivering large-scale, high-traffic distributed systems, including deep proficiency in backend service design, API development (gRPC, MCP, GraphQL), and scalable microservices architectures.
[...]
- Experience building or integrating with agentic AI systems including MCP servers, LLM orchestration layers, RAG pipelines, HITL workflows, or evaluation and guardrail frameworks for production agentic workloads, with proficiency in Go and willingness to work across Python.
[...]
- Utilizes generative AI responsibly, maintaining human oversight to deliver business-ready outputs and drive measurable improvements in workflow efficiency, cost, and quality.
[...]

results.title: Staff Site Reliability Engineer, Core AI Infrastructure at Coinbase - United States Remote
highlights:
Staff Site Reliability Engineer, Core AI Infrastructure at Coinbase - United States Remote
[...]
# Staff Site Reliability Engineer, Core AI Infrastructure
[...]
### 📋 Description
[...]
• Own the reliability, monitoring, and incident response lifecycle for AI infrastructure services, including on-call support for AWS deployment pipelines, root cause analysis, and blameless retros. • Build automation and tooling to streamline operational IT workflows, eliminate manual tasks, and improve deployment velocity across CI/CD frameworks and Kubernetes environments. • Partner with the Coinbase Infrastructure team to extend CI/CD frameworks supporting IT services and enterprise network platforms, and with Security and Compliance to integrate surveillance tooling into deployment pipelines. • Strengthen observability and documentation standards across IT engineering by defining metrics, implementing monitoring solutions, and maintaining technical documentation that sets a standard of excellence. • Develop full-stack applications that power internal AI products and infrastructure with Go or Python.
[...]
### 🎯 Requirements
[...]
• 8+ years of experience automating and supporting cloud infrastructure (AWS) and network environments, with hands-on use of infrastructure-as-code tools (Terraform, Ansible, Chef, Puppet, or Salt). • Proven experience deploying, managing, and troubleshooting containerized workloads using Docker and Kubernetes in production environments. • Proficiency in at least one scripting or programming language (Python, Bash, Ruby, or Go) and version control workflows using Git-based CI/CD pipelines. • Track record of leading incident response in environments with strict SLAs, including root cause analysis, blameless retros, and measurable reliability improvements. • Utilizes generative AI responsibly, maintaining human oversight to deliver business-ready outputs and drive measurable improvements in workflow efficiency, cost, and quality.
[...]
⛑ DevOps &
[...]
🤖 Artificial Intelligence
[...]
Site Reliability and Software Engineering leader managing NVIDIA's DGX Cloud computing services. Overseeing team operations and driving technical project success in innovative environment.

# Principal Software Engineer, AI Automation
[...]
Posted Jun

- At Coinbase, our mission is to increase economic freedom in the world. To achieve this, we are becoming an "AI-first" company and fundamentally reimagining how a global financial institution operates. AI is at the center of this strategy, driving our core processes while human experts serve as the "function calls" that the system consults for high-level judgment.
[...]
- We are looking for a visionary, AI-forward Principal Software Engineer to serve as the technical North Star for our AI Automation efforts. You will build Agentic AI systems that will power our multiple org-wide initiatives and their primitives, orchestration layers, and the tools that will enable every team at Coinbase to automate their workflows. You will report to the VP, Engineering and will operate with the highest level of autonomy to define what a fully AI-driven company looks like from the ground up.
[...]
- Architect Agentic Systems: Design and build sophisticated multi-agent architectures that move beyond simple chat to autonomous execution and complex reasoning.
[...]
- Solve the "Hard Problems" of AI: Define and implement reliable evaluation frameworks, eliminate hallucinations through rigorous data grounding, and build deep observability into LLM reasoning chains.
[...]
- Build the Core AI Platform: Develop the underlying primitives and orchestration tools that will allow the rest of the org to adopt AI, improving developer productivity and operational efficiency across the entire organization.
[...]
- Lead the AI-First Transition: Partner with leadership to identify high-impact automation opportunities across Product Foundations and Core functions, ensuring our AI strategy creates compounding leverage.
[...]
- Bridge Engineering and AI: Act as a "Software Engineer with deep AI expertise"—applying world-class backend engineering principles (scalability, reliability, performance) to the bleeding edge of Generative AI.
[...]
- Mentor and Elevate: Serve as a mentor to senior ICs and engineering teams, raising the bar for AI literacy and
[...]
- Deep AI/LLM Expertise: Extensive experience working with LLMs, RAG, and Agentic frameworks. You understand the nuances of prompt engineering, fine-tuning, and the current limitations of frontier models.
[...]
- Backend Engineering Mastery: You are a seasoned Software Engineer at heart. You can build the robust, scalable systems required to put AI into production at a global scale.
[...]
- Visionary Mindset: You are 18 months ahead of the industry curve. You can see how AI will transform business processes and have the technical chops to build that future today.
[...]
- Focus on Reliability: A passion for solving the "unsolved" problems of AI: evaluation, grounding, and deterministic outcomes in non-deterministic systems.
[...]
- Multi-Agentic Architecture: Specific experience building or researching systems where multiple AI agents collaborate to solve complex tasks.
[...]
- Open Source Contributions: Active contributions to the AI/ML community or major LLM frameworks (LangChain, AutoGPT, etc.).
[...]

results.title: Quantitative Risk Modeling Manager | Wfh
highlights:
# Quantitative Risk Modeling Manager
[...]
As part of Coinbase's broader risk management organization, the Risk Quant team is responsible for building quantitative models that assess and mitigate risks associated with our risk-bearing products, as well as measuring and monitoring portfolio exposures across the platform. We are looking for a Senior Quantitative Researcher (IC) to lead the design of Coinbase's next-generation Global Liquidation Engine.
[...]
In this role, you will not only manage risk; you will architect the automated machinery that protects the platform during extreme volatility. You will build the algorithms responsible for liquidating distressed portfolios across Spot and Derivatives (Futures, Options) at a global scale. This engine must optimize for aggregated risk reduction, trade slippage, and liquidity fragmentation across multiple venues simultaneously.
[...]

results.title: Senior Blockchain Security Engineer, Cryptography Research | Wfh
highlights:
# Senior Blockchain Security Engineer, Cryptography Research
[...]
Posted Jun 8, 2026
[...]
- Security is a primary competency at Coinbase, and the Security Team keeps a watchful eye over every aspect of it. Every day, we go to battle against some of the most sophisticated attackers in the world to protect billions of dollars worth of digital assets and ensure that our customers and employees can enjoy a safe, trusted experience. As Coinbase scales globally, our team is scaling along with it, using a blend of tooling, automation, and strategic team growth to ensure that we’re well-equipped to protect the next billion users of crypto.
[...]
Blockchain Security (BlockSec) team’s mission is to Secure funds and data from protocol through wallet. Coinbase has a unique opportunity: enabling our customers to easily and securely interface with blockchains, a.k.a. digital asset networks, a.k.a. cryptocurrencies. Blockchains are a cutting edge technology, with many unique security considerations for any entity that supports interaction with them. Understanding those unique considerations requires deep expertise in blockchains and the ability to maintain that expertise over time.
[...]
What you’ll be doing (ie. job duties):
[...]
The mission of the BlockSec Research and Engineering team is to enable the whole blockchain security org to operate on the cutting edge of solving crypto security problems. We expect to accomplish this with a multi-pronged strategy, including identifying and analyzing risks of emerging trends in Blockchain Security, developing minimum viable products to improve Blockchain Security team’s performance, understanding new risks posed by novel crypto asset design, and ensuring we have a pipeline to apply all these learnings to designing and maintaining Coinbase backend engineering systems.
[...]
This role includes:
[...]
- Analysis of advanced cryptographic techniques like multi-party computation (MPC), zero-knowledge proofs (ZK), etc. used in the design of certain blockchain technologies, related backend systems, and new Coinbase products. Providing critical feedback on their use, help understand the risks they pose, and how to handle these risks systematically.
[...]
- Analysis of complex blockchain protocols and emerging blockchain technologies like staking, bridges, rollups, etc., especially from a cryptographic perspective. Providing a risk assessment of them, how to manage and mitigate the risks, etc.
[...]
- Staying on the cutting edge w/ Blockchain Security knowledge by identifying trends in Blockchain security using external research in the industry, new technological developments, security incidents, etc.
[...]
- What we look for in you (ie. job requirements):
[...]
- 7+ years of experience in working with advanced cryptographic techniques like multi-party computation (MPC), zero-knowledge proofs (ZK), verifiable random/delay functions (VRF, VDF), threshold techniques, etc.
- 3+ years of experience with the blockchain ecosystem, in particular with new consensus mechanisms and emerging tech
[...]
- Willingness to research BlockSec risks and risk identification and mitigation methods to develop repeatable innovation
[...]
- Knowledge of engineering/tooling and ability to quickly develop working prototypes
[...]
- Ability to investigate a new topic through white papers or blog posts
[...]
- Ability to communicate with technical and non-technical stakeholders in order to drive alignment
[...]
- You have an energy and self-drive for continuous learning as Crypto is a constantly and rapidly changing space
[...]
- Ability to do both long term thinking and short term planning
[...]
- Nice to haves:
[...]
- An advanced degree like PhD in cryptography, mathematics, or a related field (number of years spent doing cryptography research in PhD can be counted towards the first point in the job requirements listed above)
- Publications in crypto/blockchain conferences

results.title: 20 AI Jobs Companies Are Hiring For in 2026 - Open Data Science - Your News Source for AI, Machine Learning & more
highlights:
For two years, the dominant story about AI and work has been job losses. Meta and Coinbase recently cut staff and named AI as one of the reasons. Past headlines warned that programmers and engineering managers would be the first to go.
[...]
The story at Box is different. As The New York Times reported in June 2026, the Silicon Valley software company has created 13 new kinds of roles because of AI, with titles like AI architect, AI solutions manager, and AI platform leader.
[...]
Here are 20 roles that companies are hiring for now, drawn from the Box reporting and from labor data gathered by LinkedIn, Lightcast, and others. Some are genuinely new. Others are familiar jobs rebranded for an AI era. The distinction matters, so this article notes which is which.
[...]
As the core builder, AI engineers design, train, and ship the models and systems a company runs on. According to LinkedIn data cited by The Wall Street Journal, this title accounts for much of the roughly 640,000 new U.S. jobs associated with AI between 2023 and 2025. It is the most established role on this list and the closest thing to a safe bet.
[...]
A step above the engineer in scope. The architect decides how AI systems fit together across a company: which models go where, how data flows between them, how the pieces hold up as the technology shifts underneath. Box created this role specifically.
[...]
An engineer who sits with the customer rather than at headquarters. Box is hiring these to help clients who want to use AI but lack the technical staff to wire it up themselves. The role borrows from a model that firms like Palantir made well known, now spreading because so many buyers are AI-curious and implementation-poor. A related role is Forward Deployed AI Engineer
[...]
AI business automation engineer
[...]
Someone whose full-time job is measuring how well models perform so customers can choose the right one for a task. Box hired Sidharth Srinivasan, 23, into exactly this role straight out of Stanford. He noted that if he had described “AI evals” to himself two years earlier, he would not have known what the words meant. Few roles capture the speed of this shift better.
[...]
integrate AI into
[...]
### AI solutions manager
[...]
Owns the path from a customer’s problem to a working AI answer.
[...]
created this role. It is a project-management discipline reshaped around the particular messiness of deploying systems that behave probabilistically rather than predictably.
[...]
### AI trainer
[...]
Shapes what data a model sees and how it behaves. As
[...]
fine-tune systems on their own data and norms, someone has to curate that input and judge the output. The role sits at the boundary between technical work and editorial judgment.
[...]
### AI accelerator
[...]
A role rooted
[...]
actual job listings rather than speculation, focused
[...]
driving AI adoption fast
[...]
accelerator is the person who
[...]
an AI initiative
[...]
stalling after the pilot,
[...]
part evangelist and part internal consultant.
[...]
### AI operations manager
[...]
Not every job in the AI economy will be one of the new titles listed above. Demand for traditional roles is expected to stay strong, in some cases very strong. The Bureau of Labor Statistics projects 34 percent employment growth for data scientists between 2024 and 2034, much faster than the average for all occupations, and CompTIA expects tech occupations overall to grow at roughly twice the rate of the broader U.S. workforce over the next decade. Data scientists, data engineers, machine learning engineers, and software engineers remain core to how companies build and run AI. The new roles are expected to sit alongside these established ones rather than replacing them.

results.title: What Will Be AI’s Biggest Bottleneck? Coinbase CEO Gives His Take
highlights:
# What Will Be AI’s Biggest Bottleneck? Coinbase CEO Gives His Take
[...]
What Will Be AI’s Biggest Bottleneck? Coinbase CEO Gives His Take
[...]
# What Will Be AI’s Biggest Bottleneck? Coinbase CEO Gives His Take
[...]
- Brian Armstrong says energy and compute, not model quality, will cap AI growth.
- He predicts 80% of AI workloads will shift to 99% cheaper models within 18 months.
- Coinbase uses prompt routing to hold AI costs flat as token usage grows exponentially.
[...]
Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong argues that energy and compute infrastructure, not model quality, will define the upper limits of artificial intelligence growth.
[...]
Armstrong made the observation in reply to a post by investor Tommy Shaughnessy, who outlined how metered API pricing is pushing enterprise AI spend well beyond what flat-rate subscriptions led companies to expect.
[...]
## Demand for Intelligence Is Near Infinite
[...]
The Coinbase CEO’s core argument is that the appetite for AI-generated intelligence has no practical ceiling.
[...]
However, he expects the market to divide sharply within 12 to 18 months. Around 80% of workloads, he predicts, will migrate to models priced up to 99% below current top-tier options.
[...]
Good takeMy guess is– demand for intelligence is near infinite– but 80% of workloads will be running on 99% cheaper models within 12-18 months– 20% of workloads will still run on latest gen models where IQ maxing is important (scientific breakthroughs, higher level… https://t.co/gAFtYjorRh
[...]
The remaining 20%, covering use cases where peak performance matters, such as scientific research or high-level orchestrator agents, will continue running on frontier models.
[...]
Armstrong compared the split to consumer hardware, noting that most buyers skip maxed-out specs on MacBooks and gaming PCs, even as prices fall faster than Moore’s Law would predict.
[...]
He added that this compression will not resolve the scarcity problem. As model costs drop and cheap alternatives proliferate, the bottleneck simply shifts upstream. It moves to the power and silicon required to run any model at scale.
[...]
## Coinbase’s Routing Strategy
[...]
Coinbase is already applying this logic in practice. Armstrong said the exchange routes prompt to lower-cost models where appropriate, keeping AI spend roughly flat even as token usage grows exponentially.
[...]
His Coinbase AI-native restructuring earlier in 2026 signaled a broader shift toward efficient, agent-driven workflows. His stance against AI overregulation reflects confidence that the technology’s trajectory should not be constrained by policy.
[...]
Shaughnessy also noted that open-source models such as DeepSeek V4 perform within the range of top proprietary systems at roughly one-thirtieth the cost, placing a ceiling on what frontier labs can charge.
[...]
## Energy as the Binding Constraint
[...]
Armstrong’s conclusion is that model quality will converge while cheaper alternatives close the performance gap. The real limit, he says, will be the physical infrastructure powering every tier of AI deployment.
[...]
That view aligns with capital flows already visible in the market. AI venture funding in Q1 2026 reached $242 billion globally, yet data center capacity is already stalling against demand.
[...]
Armstrong’s point is not which model prevails, but whether energy and computing infrastructure can keep pace with demand that, by his own assessment, has no natural ceiling.

results.title: [AI WEEKLY NEWS RUNDOWN] The Labor Squeeze, Apple's Intel Deal, and OpenAI's Smartphone (May 03 - 10 2026)
highlights:
Summary: In this weekly briefing, we analyze the “Automation Squeeze.” We deconstruct the massive wave of white-collar layoffs as Cloudflare and Coinbase cut up to 20% of their staff to implement “agentic AI-first” operations. We explore the fracturing hardware supply chain, highlighted by Apple’s preliminary chip deal with Intel and a collapse in global motherboard sales due to soaring RAM prices. We also dive into the courtroom drama of Mira Murati testifying against Sam Altman, the surprising compute truce between Elon Musk’s SpaceX and Anthropic, OpenAI’s push to build 30 million AI smartphones by 2027, and a landmark Harvard study proving AI can outperform ER doctors in diagnostic triage.
[...]
The Agentic Labor Purge: Cloudflare cuts 1,100 jobs (20%) and Coinbase cuts 700 jobs (14%), explicitly citing the shift to AI-driven productivity and flattened org charts.
[...]
Find REMOTE AI Jobs (Mercor): Apply Here
[...]
## Coinbase cuts 14% of staff citing AI
[...]
Coinbase is laying off roughly 700 workers, about 14% of its staff, as CEO Brian Armstrong pushes a restructuring tied to crypto market volatility and a bigger push to use AI tools across the company.
[...]
The reorg flattens the company to five layers below the CEO and COO, lets leaders manage more than 15 direct reports, and asks managers to contribute more work themselves rather than just oversee others.
[...]
Coinbase will try “one-person teams” blending engineering, design, and product management roles using AI tools, and expects to take $50 million to $60 million in severance costs, according to an SEC filing.

results.title: Coinbase Cuts Workforce to Go AI Native | Beenco Labs posted on the topic | LinkedIn
highlights:
# Coinbase Cuts Workforce to Go AI Native
[...]
AI is compressing company structure in real time. Coinbase cuts 14% of its workforce to go “AI Native.” Smaller teams, faster execution, and AI agents already handling real onchain transactions. Web3 is becoming the economic layer for AI systems that need instant, trustless settlement. Builders integrating AI with onchain logic are positioning for the next massive cycle.
[...]
This is an architectural rebuild. 🏗️ Coinbase is cutting deep to become AI-native, and the blueprint is ruthless: • 📉 𝗙𝗹𝗮𝘁𝘁𝗲𝗻𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗛𝗶𝗲𝗿𝗮𝗿𝗰𝗵𝘆: Max 5 layers below the CEO. • 🚫 𝗡𝗼 "𝗣𝘂𝗿𝗲 𝗠𝗮𝗻𝗮𝗴𝗲𝗿𝘀": The era of the "traffic controller" is over. • 🏀 𝗣𝗹𝗮𝘆𝗲𝗿 𝗖𝗼𝗮𝗰𝗵𝗲𝘀: If you lead, you also build. Coding is mandatory. • The "blast radius" is here. Is your organization designed to survive it? 🌍 🎥 Watch the full breakdown and hit Subscribe for your weekly tech edge! 🔔 https://lnkd.in/d_SvSNMy#Alitalkstech#Coinbase#AI#FutureOfWork#TechLeadership#AfricaTech Ali Hussein Kassim Abraham Muthogo Kamau Rose Muturi Harry Hare
[...]
Coinbase just cut 14% of its workforce… and CEO Brian Armstrong says it may be one of the best decisions they’ve made. His reasoning comes down to two things: The market and AI. Armstrong says crypto volatility and the current down market forced the company to operate leaner. But the bigger signal is AI. In his words, “AI is changing how we work.” The idea is simple: fewer layers, faster decisions, and a more efficient company built around automation. That’s the shift happening across tech right now. AI isn’t just changing products anymore… it’s changing company structures. Swipe to read Armstrong’s full reasoning. What do you think? 🤔💬#ai#ainews#aiupdates
[...]
The transition toward AI-native organizations is beginning to reshape workforce strategies across the tech sector. Coinbase￼ reportedly reduced its workforce by 14% as part of a strategic effort to increase AI integration across operations and workflows. The development reflects a broader industry trend where companies are leveraging AI to streamline processes, enhance productivity, and optimize operational efficiency. As AI adoption expands, organizations are increasingly evaluating how automation and human expertise can be combined within future business models. ✅Follow @nexautech For more AI Info and Contents#ArtificialIntelligence#FutureOfWork#Innovation#Coinbase#Technology
[...]
Coinbase's Base launches AI tool for ChatGPT to manage crypto wallets and DeFi apps: Coinbase's Ethereum Layer 2 network Base launched “Base MCP,” a new AI integration that lets users connect their crypto wallets to AI tools like ...
[...]
Connecting AI agents to real on-chain liquidity isn't just theory anymore—it's dangerous. Most teams just slap an LLM onto a wallet API and hope for the best. But on-chain, the margin for error is zero. One hallucinated decision, and the funds are gone. If agents are going to move serious capital, the execution layer must be unbreakable. Here is how it actually needs to be built: - Secure Rails: Use Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs) to guarantee the agent's logic runs exactly as intended without tampering. - Strict Boundaries: Never give a bot open-ended power. Enforce time-locks, spending limits, and multi-sig approvals. - Deterministic Execution: Smart contracts with hard invariants and strict rejection conditions. The contract enforces what is allowed, rather than guessing what the model "intended." The market is obsessed with getting AI to transact. We are focused on building the deterministic infrastructure that lets them transact safely. Let's build your AI integration layer the right way. By Dmitriy Babenko

results.title: Coinbase MCP Integration with LlamaIndex | Composio
highlights:
# How to integrate Coinbase MCP with LlamaIndex
[...]
This guide walks you through connecting Coinbase to LlamaIndex using the Composio tool router. By the end, you'll have a working Coinbase agent that can show all your coinbase wallet balances, list every crypto wallet i own, display recent wallets added to your account through natural language commands.
[...]
This guide will help you understand how to give your LlamaIndex agent real control over a Coinbase account through Composio's Coinbase MCP server.
[...]
- Set your OpenAI and Composio API keys
- Install LlamaIndex and Composio packages
- Create a Composio Tool Router session for Coinbase
- Connect LlamaIndex to the Coinbase MCP server
- Build a Coinbase-powered agent using LlamaIndex
- Interact with Coinbase through natural language
[...]
LlamaIndex is a data framework for building LLM applications. It provides tools for connecting LLMs to external data sources and services through agents and tools.
[...]
The Coinbase MCP server is an implementation of the Model Context Protocol that connects your AI agent and assistants like Claude, Cursor, etc directly to your Coinbase account. It provides structured and secure access to your cryptocurrency wallets, so your agent can retrieve wallet balances, monitor your digital assets, and help you stay informed about your crypto holdings in real time.
[...]
The Coinbase MCP server is an implementation of the Model Context Protocol that connects your AI agent to Coinbase. It provides structured and secure access so your agent can perform Coinbase operations on your behalf through a secure, permission-based interface.
[...]
With Composio's managed implementation, you don't have to create your own developer app. For production, if you're building an end product, we recommend using your own credentials. The managed server helps you prototype fast and go from 0-1 faster.
[...]
- We create a Composio client using your API key and configure it with the LlamaIndex provider
- We then create a tool router MCP session for your user, specifying the toolkits we want to use (in this case, coinbase)
- The session returns an MCP HTTP endpoint URL that acts as a gateway to all your configured tools
- LlamaIndex will connect to this endpoint to dynamically discover and use the available Coinbase tools.
- The MCP tools are mapped to LlamaIndex-compatible tools and plug them into the Agent.

[...]

[...]

[...]
You've successfully connected Coinbase to LlamaIndex through Composio's Tool Router MCP layer.
[...]
- Tool Router dynamically exposes Coinbase tools through an MCP endpoint
- LlamaIndex's ReActAgent handles reasoning and orchestration; Composio handles integrations
- The agent becomes more capable without increasing prompt size
- Async Python provides clean, efficient execution of agent workflows
[...]
With a standalone Coinbase MCP server, the agents and LLMs can only access a fixed set of Coinbase tools tied to that server. However, with the Composio Tool Router, agents can dynamically load tools from Coinbase and many other apps based on the task at hand, all through a single MCP endpoint.
[...]
Yes, you can. LlamaIndex fully supports MCP integration. You get structured tool calling, message history handling, and model orchestration while Tool Router takes care of discovering and serving the right Coinbase tools.
